contract.goal: Move the standalone review-skill plugin's single SKILL.md into the commons plugin's skills tree and remove review-skill as a top-level marketplace entry. The commons plugin already names interview/review skills in its description (set by PR1 in anticipation of this fold); PR8 makes that description true. One fewer top-level marketplace plugin; same skill availability via the agent-loop-full cascade.
acceptance_criteria: plugins/commons/skills/review-skill/SKILL.md exists with the same byte content as the pre-move plugins/review-skill/skills/review-skill/SKILL.md (verified by sha256 or byte-diff).
plugins/review-skill/ directory deleted (zero files remaining; including .claude-plugin/plugin.json and the now-empty skills/ subtree).
.claude-plugin/marketplace.json plugins[] array no longer contains a top-level entry with name 'review-skill'; the array has exactly 6 entries (was 7).
.claude-plugin/marketplace.json agent-loop-full entry's dependencies array no longer contains 'review-skill'; commons remains first.
.claude-plugin/marketplace.json top-level description copy updated to drop 'review-skill' from its plugin enumeration.
.claude-plugin/marketplace.json agent-loop-full entry's description copy updated to drop 'review-skill' from the cascaded family list.
plugins/agent-loop-full/.claude-plugin/plugin.json description updated to match (drop 'review-skill' from the cascaded family list).

cli/marketplace-manifest.test.ts: the 'declares exactly the seven expected plugins' test (line ~99) renamed and updated to 'six'; the test at line ~186 asserting 'review-skill has no dependencies' removed (review-skill no longer exists as a plugin); the EXPECTED_PLUGINS-like constants at lines ~59 and ~213 reduced to the 6-plugin closed set [commons, griot, guild, loom, ev, agent-loop-full]; closed-set invariant preserved (no loosening to 'any name allowed').

scripts/sync-shared.ts PLUGINS array (line ~67) drops 'review-skill'; the anticipatory comments at ~lines 74 and 89-91 updated to reflect the now-completed fold (or rewritten to drop the past-tense anticipation).
scripts/sync-shared.test.ts updated: fixture write at line ~71 dropped (root skills/review-skill fixture is now meaningless); the plugin loops at lines ~292 and ~426-427 reduced to the 6-plugin set.
README.md line ~22 plugin table row for `review-skill@krambuhl` removed; line ~41 cascade-order reference to review-skill removed or rewritten.
npm test passes (all suites green, including the marketplace-manifest closed-set assertion and sync-shared drift suite).
node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check passes (no drift; review-skill removal does not invalidate any active sync direction since COMMONS_CONSUMERS already excluded it).
rules_applied: PLAN.md § PR8 — fold review-skill into commons; standalone plugin disappears; agent-loop-full cascade no longer names it.
Closed-set marketplace invariant — the manifest test must continue to assert an EXACT list of plugins (no loosening to 'any name allowed'). Just shrink the set from 7 to 6.

Dependency ordering (PLAN.md § PR1 + design-systems whiteboard finding) — commons-first in agent-loop-full's dependencies array; substrate-kind precedes peer-kind. After dropping review-skill the order is [commons, griot, guild, loom, ev].
Don't touch root skills/review-skill/SKILL.md — it's a pre-PR4 relic that becomes dead weight with this PR. PR9 deletes root skills/ entirely; sweeping it here would conflate two PR boundaries.

PR scope discipline — single conceptual unit (consolidation of one plugin into another). Mechanical fan-out across marketplace.json + 2 plugin.json files + 2 test files + 1 sync script + 1 README is mechanically uniform around one decision.

Substrate-gap finding from this unit's setup: bin/loom has no `phase add` / `phase create` verb. Phases must already exist in manifest.json before `phase update` can write to them. Worked around by direct manifest edit; captured as a substrate-improvement candidate for a future loom-extension project.
disqualifiers: Touching anything under root cli/, root skills/, root docs/, root agents/ — that's PR9's territory (the canonical-root dissolution).
Touching plugins/commons/skills/ for any skill other than review-skill (grill-me and find-skills landed in PR5; their bodies are untouched here).
Changing the SKILL.md content of review-skill (PR8 is relocation + plumbing; not a skill rewrite).
Loosening any closed-set marketplace assertion to 'any name allowed' (the tripwire is load-bearing per PR1's design).
Adding new tests (PR8 is a shrink; existing tests update to the smaller set, no new coverage authored).
Backfilling Phase 2 entries in projects/2026-05-21-repo-compartmentalize/manifest.json (Phase 3 was added forward-only this session; touching Phase 2 retroactively is out-of-scope).
Drift on `node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check` after the change.
inputs: plugins/commons/skills/ (destination directory; sibling of existing grill-me and find-skills)
.claude-plugin/marketplace.json (top-level description + plugins array + agent-loop-full dependencies and description)

actions: Synced local main to origin/main (was 2 commits behind after PR #24 merge of PR7).
Cut new branch repo-compartmentalize.cleanup from updated main.

Manually appended Phase 3 entry to projects/2026-05-21-repo-compartmentalize/manifest.json (loom CLI has no `phase add` verb; `phase update` requires the phase to pre-exist). Then ran `loom phase update repo-compartmentalize 3 --status=in-progress --branch=repo-compartmentalize.cleanup` to fire phase-started event.

Wrote contract-only checkin 08 via `loom checkin write` (substrate enforces checkin immutability via writeCheckin's checkin-already-exists guard; the eventual execution/verdict update at unit end edits the file directly — flagged as substrate gap below).
Showed user the PR8 contract via AskUserQuestion (one question, 3 options: approve / sweep root relic too / keep lighter touch). User approved as drafted.
Moved plugins/review-skill/skills/review-skill/SKILL.md → plugins/commons/skills/review-skill/SKILL.md (byte-equal; mkdir + mv).
Deleted plugins/review-skill/ (rm -rf).

Edited .claude-plugin/marketplace.json: dropped 'review-skill' from top-level description string; deleted the review-skill plugins[] entry (7 → 6 entries); removed 'review-skill' from agent-loop-full's dependencies array; updated agent-loop-full's description string to drop 'review-skill' from the family enumeration.
Edited plugins/agent-loop-full/.claude-plugin/plugin.json: dropped 'review-skill' from description string.

Edited cli/marketplace-manifest.test.ts: shrunk EXPECTED_PLUGIN_NAMES from 7 to 6 (line 53-60); renamed test 'declares exactly the seven expected plugins' → 'six' (line 99); deleted the standalone 'review-skill has no dependencies' test (was line 186-188); renamed agent-loop-full deps test from 'all six family' → 'all five family' and shrunk its expected array to the 5-plugin set (line 206-214).

Edited scripts/sync-shared.ts: removed 'review-skill' from PLUGINS const array (line 67); updated the PLUGINS_WITH_CLI comment to drop the example reference to review-skill (line 74); updated the COMMONS_CONSUMERS doc-comment to remove the anticipatory 'review-skill folds into commons in a future PR' prose (lines 89-91).

Edited scripts/sync-shared.test.ts: removed the root skills/review-skill/SKILL.md fixture write (line 71); shrunk the plugin loop at line 292 from 5 plugins to 4 (dropped 'review-skill'); shrunk the plugin loop at line 427 from 2 plugins to 1 (dropped 'review-skill', kept 'agent-loop-full') and updated the surrounding comment.

Edited README.md: updated the 'ships as seven' → 'ships as six' install copy at line 13; deleted the `review-skill@krambuhl` plugin table row at line 22 and updated the agent-loop-full row's 'all six above' → 'all five above'; updated the cascade-order narrative at lines 40-41 to drop the trailing 'review-skill'.

Verified npm test → 1125/1125 passing across 75 test files (1133→1125 = 8 tests removed: 1 standalone review-skill-has-no-deps + 7 sub-tests under describe('entry: review-skill') iterating EXPECTED_PLUGIN_NAMES; evaluator-test-unit later recounted these as 6 sub-tests + 1 standalone = 7, not 8, which is an off-by-one in the artifact summary but didn't block the panel since the runtime signal is green).
Verified node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check → ok (4 plugins synced: griot, guild, loom, ev).

Verified grep for stale 'review-skill' references in active code surface — only one remaining hit: root skills/review-skill/SKILL.md (a pre-PR4 root relic; PR9 deletes root skills/ entirely; correctly left untouched per disqualifier 1).
Ran panel auto-derivation via `guild derive-panel --files=...` → evaluator-contract-fit, evaluator-react-api, evaluator-test-unit, evaluator-naming.
Spawned 4 evaluators in parallel via /guild-spawn; all 4 returned VERDICT: approved with zero findings.
Aggregated via `guild parse-and-aggregate` → verdict: approved, blocking_findings: [], advisory_findings: [], cli_runs: [], conflicts: [].
Scope-shift detection: 0 signals (panel clean; user said 'keep going'; no whiteboard; not at phase boundary). No inner-RPI.
files_touched: .claude-plugin/marketplace.json (top-level description; plugins[] entry removed; agent-loop-full description + dependencies)
cli/marketplace-manifest.test.ts (EXPECTED_PLUGIN_NAMES; 'six expected' test name; review-skill-has-no-deps test deleted; 'five family' assertion shrunk)
scripts/sync-shared.ts (PLUGINS array; PLUGINS_WITH_CLI comment; COMMONS_CONSUMERS comment)
scripts/sync-shared.test.ts (fixture write removed; two plugin loops shrunk; comment updated)
README.md (install copy plugin count; table row removed; agent-loop-full row 'all five above'; cascade-order narrative)
projects/2026-05-21-repo-compartmentalize/manifest.json (substrate — Phase 3 entry appended forward-only; not part of PR8 artifact)
projects/2026-05-21-repo-compartmentalize/events.jsonl (substrate — phase-update + checkin-created events auto-written by loom verbs)

changes_since_previous: PR7 (delete orphan learnings/) merged to main as PR #24 at e690812. Phase 2 closed. Phase 3 (Cleanup) starts now. New branch repo-compartmentalize.cleanup cut from updated main. Manifest updated forward-only to include Phase 3 entry (`loom phase update` requires the phase to already exist; no `phase add` verb today, which is captured as a substrate gap in rules_applied).
notes_for_pr: Phase 3 (Cleanup) begins here. 4 PRs total: PR8 (this one), PR9 (delete root cli/skills/agents/docs/), PR10 (delete root bin/ shims + install-sh tripwire), PR11 (prose sweep).

Substrate gap surfaced: bin/loom has no `phase add` / `phase create` verb. Phases must already exist in manifest.json before `phase update` can write status/branch to them. Worked around by direct manifest edit (Phase 3 entry appended forward-only; Phase 2 NOT backfilled — Phase 2's work happened on repo-compartmentalize.bulk branch without phase entries through PRs 4-7, and retroactive backfill is out-of-scope). Captured for a future loom-extension project.

Substrate gap surfaced (second): writeCheckin enforces immutability (checkin-already-exists throws), but ev-loop-interactive's Step 2.1 framing ('write contract-only, finalize later') implies mutability. Project precedent (PR7's checkin) is 'write complete checkin once at unit end.' For PR8 the contract-only checkin written at unit start was edited directly at unit end with execution + verdict — bypassing the verb layer. Either the skill body needs revision to match the immutable storage layer, or writeCheckin needs an --update affordance. Captured for a substrate-improvement followup.

Test count delta accounting: 1133 → 1125 = 8 tests gone. evaluator-test-unit recounted as 1 standalone + 6 sub-tests = 7 (off by one against the artifact summary's 8). Not flagged as blocking since runtime signal is green; the recount discrepancy is a curiosity worth resolving if PR9 also surfaces it.

Fresh-install verification deferred: PLAN PR8 § Verification says 'install agent-loop-full@krambuhl on a fresh machine — /review-skill invocation resolves via commons rather than the old standalone plugin.' That step can't be done from inside the repo; the structural prerequisites are in place (commons owns the skill, marketplace + cascade point at commons, sync-shared still green). evaluator-contract-fit flagged this as not-a-blocker.

Root relic NOT swept: skills/review-skill/SKILL.md at the repo root still exists (a pre-PR4 relic). PR9 deletes root skills/ entirely; sweeping in PR8 was rejected at contract-approve time to keep PR boundaries clean.
Panel: evaluator-contract-fit, evaluator-react-api, evaluator-test-unit, evaluator-naming — all approved, zero findings.
Whiteboard skipped for Phase 3 (project precedent from Phase 2; PR8-11 are mechanical cleanups; PLAN.md has no `**Whiteboard**:` override block under Phase 3).
